sources_exist.note: Ten of 12 URL occurrences resolved to accessible pages. The ALC survey URL returned HTTP 402 and the Taylor & Francis DOI returned HTTP 403, so those two citations were inaccessible under the validator's no-inaccessible-source rule.

figures_match_sources.note: Slator supports the 33% current-adoption figure and 29%-versus-11% ALC change; Weglot and Nimdzi support discounted MTPE pricing; and HHS supports a healthcare language-access mandate. The cited pages do not establish six active consolidators, the 5.5x buy median, the quoted $147M LanguageLine EBITDA or 10.2x exit multiple, the CAT/NMT diffusion timelines, the 25% owner-age estimate, Weglot's claimed light/full MTPE percentage tiers, the interpreting-contract re-rating claim, or most non-healthcare moat gates.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The declared fractions are within bounds and the translation/interpreting segmentation is directionally sensible. However, inputs.ai_replaceable_share is an entirely hypothesized LOW-quality W-2 role mix with no cited LSP wage decomposition; pi_dist=0.30 relies partly on unsupported hospital/public-payer interpreting re-rating; and pi_moat=0.45 combines healthcare, court, USCIS, sworn, ISO, HIPAA, and bypass assertions without evidence for most components. Most importantly, t50_years=1.5 is years from today until 50% of firms adopt, yet its derivation says the current mid-2026 share is plausibly already at or above 50%; those statements cannot both define the same threshold.

rubric_consistent.note: Identity and non-null deterministic dataset inputs match, and the frozen score formulas are applied as written. The LOW_CONFIDENCE flag is explicit and invokes the confidence cap; S=3.874953 is 0.1250 below the 4.00 conditional cut, resolving the assigned BORDERLINE flag, while MELTING separately caps the grade at pass. Nevertheless, the internally inconsistent t50 definition and unsupported B/M/C research inputs are score-affecting and prevent rubric-consistent acceptance.

cross_checks_honest.note: The record candidly separates gross uplift from capture, identifies the contaminated 5419 role proxy, confines deal evidence to B/M, and distinguishes melting written translation from mandate-protected interpreting. But the terminal check quantifies a one-third-to-one-half protected revenue floor without evidence, and the pricing check repeats an unsupported claim that hospital procurement and public payers re-rate interpreting contracts downward; those claims materially overstate the evidence behind terminal value and C.

source_audits.note: The article supports Propio's September 2024 ULG acquisition, Leonard Green's investment, and the later Akorbi/ASL acquisitions. It does not document the other five named platforms or establish value=6 PE-backed consolidators with qualifying US add-ons in the required window.

The article reports Into23 revenue of roughly $0.75M, a price of roughly $0.81M for 51%, and a 6.3x EBITDA multiple. It does not report the claimed approximately 5x Stoquart transaction or a recent boutique median of 5.5x.

Slator reports an April 2024 survey of 223 companies in which one in three LSPs had implemented LLMs, close to 20% had tested use cases or betas, 3% had no plans, and adoption was 75% for Super Agencies versus just under 30% for Boutiques.

The article supports the $1.5B acquisition price and LanguageLine's $388M 2015 revenue. It reports EUR 41M EBITDA on EUR 114M revenue contributed after closing, not the quoted $147M adjusted EBITDA, so the cited page does not establish a 10.2x EV/EBITDA multiple.

The article directly supports 29% of 70 MT-using companies using LLMs for MT in 2024 versus 11% in 2023. It does not support the claimed decade-long CAT/TM transition, the five-to-seven-year NMT-majority timeline, or their use as the historical analog underlying t50.

The exact page returned HTTP 402 and the indexed public material only describes how to purchase or obtain the survey. No accessible owner-age statistic supports value=0.25, and the record's separate Census ABS premise has no cited URL.

Weglot states that MTPE rates usually fall between 30% and 70% of a standard human translation rate and gives per-word, project, and hourly pricing examples, supporting translation-side price pass-through.

Nimdzi describes MTPE cost savings, light-editing discounts up to 35%-40%, per-word pricing as most common, and MT-discount grids used by LSPs. It supports translation-side pass-through but says nothing about hospital or public-payer interpreting-contract renewals.
The exact full-text DOI could not be accessed and the PDF endpoint returned HTTP 403. No accessible content could be verified for the record's price-deflation or interpreting-contract claims.

HHS Section 1557 materials establish nondiscrimination and meaningful-access obligations that can require language assistance and competent interpreters in covered healthcare settings. This supports the healthcare mandate component, not the separate court, USCIS, sworn-translation, ISO, or HIPAA assertions.

The accessible report page provides market size, broad descriptions of managed multilingual outcomes and human expertise, and a table of contents mentioning US language access and legal markets. It does not establish the claimed certification gates, quantify protected revenue, or show that ordinary translation clients bypass LSPs.

Weglot supports overall MTPE rates of 30%-70% of human translation and identifies per-word, per-project, and hourly models. It does not state the quoted light-post-editing rate of 40%-50% or full-post-editing rate of 50%-70%; its light/full section instead reports workflow definitions and daily output.

reasons: Re-derive inputs.t50_years using the prompt's exact definition—years from 2026-07-20 until 50% of firms materially adopt—and reconcile it with inputs.current_adoption_pct and the record's statement that adoption is plausibly already at or above 50%; add current, representative LSP evidence rather than changing the threshold definition.

Source inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role with an actual LSP W-2 wage-bill or role decomposition and separate 1099 production labor consistently; the current six-role structure is entirely hypothesized and marked LOW quality.

Correct inputs.active_consolidators.value or add source-auditable evidence for every qualifying platform; https://slator.com/propio-language-services-acquires-united-language-group/ documents Propio transactions but not six PE-backed active consolidators.

Re-source inputs.buy_mult with disclosed small-LSP transactions sufficient to establish the stated median, and re-source inputs.exit_mult with a comparable platform transaction that directly reports both enterprise value and adjusted EBITDA; the cited Into23 page supports only 6.3x and https://slator.com/teleperformance-folds-languageline-into-new-unit-keeps-brand/ does not state the quoted $147M EBITDA.

Replace the memory-based CAT/TM and NMT timelines in inputs.historical_analogs with directly cited diffusion evidence and then re-derive inputs.t50_years; https://slator.com/translation-companies-accelerating-adoption-large-language-models-alc-survey/ supports only the 2023-to-2024 LLM-use change.

Provide an accessible, directly cited owner-age benchmark and a complete transformation for inputs.owners_60plus_pct; https://www.alcus.org/page/2024IndustrySurvey is inaccessible to the validator and the uncited Census premise does not support the 25% LSP estimate.

Support or remove the hospital-procurement and public-payer interpreting re-rating mechanism in inputs.pi_dist.rationale and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation; Weglot and Nimdzi support written-translation MTPE discounts, while https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/0907676X.2023.2292572 was inaccessible and no cited source establishes interpreting-contract clawback.

Add primary evidence for the court, USCIS, sworn-translation, ISO, HIPAA, and client-bypass components of inputs.pi_moat; the HHS citation supports healthcare language access, while https://slator.com/2026-market-report-language-solutions-ai/ is only a high-level report sales page.

Correct inputs.pricing_structure.figure_quoted and cross_checks.terminal_value with source-backed figures: https://www.weglot.com/blog/machine-translation-post-editing-rates supports the overall 30%-70% MTPE range but not the stated light/full percentage tiers, and no citation supports the claimed one-third-to-one-half mandate-protected revenue floor.
